question: After SQL optimization of the data submission API, what was the peak CPU usage observed during the final 300 concurrent user stress test?

haystack_sessions.content: [EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-10-20 group=Group 3 message_index=4 time=2025-10-20 09:18:20] Xinmeng Tian: Good morning, everyone. Today, I'm also starting a new task for our team: load testing the data submission API interface. My main work today is to write JMeter scripts and prepare test data.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-10-21 group=Group 3 message_index=2 time=2025-10-21 09:19:20] Xinmeng Tian: Good morning everyone. The load testing for the data submission API will officially begin today. Yesterday, I finished preparing the JMeter scripts and basic test data. The plan is to run the first round of benchmark tests this morning with 100 concurrent users to see the system's initial performance. This task has a higher priority, so I will focus on completing it first before switching to the energy consumption monitoring project tasks.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-10-21 group=Group 3 message_index=3 time=2025-10-21 09:35:15] Huilan Chen: Received. Both plans are very clear. @Hongxin Ding, excellent! It's commendable that you're proactively addressing issues exposed during stress testing with targeted optimizations. This closed-loop working method should be promoted. @Xinmeng Tian, load testing is very important. I look forward to your first round of results, as this will provide crucial data for our subsequent performance tuning. Keep up the good work, everyone!

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-10-21 group=Group 3 message_index=6 time=2025-10-21 16:15:45] Xinmeng Tian: @all The first round of load testing (100 concurrent users) for the data submission API has been completed. Preliminary results are as follows: the average response time is around 480ms, 95% of requests are completed within 850ms, and there was a 0.5% error rate during the test. The server CPU usage peaked at 70%. Overall, it's within an acceptable range, but there's room for optimization. The report will be compiled and sent out shortly. I'm now preparing to set up the 300 concurrent user test scenario.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-10-21 group=Group 3 message_index=7 time=2025-10-21 16:25:00] Mingzhi Li: @Xinmeng Tian Received, thank you for your hard work. An average of 480ms is acceptable for a data submission interface, but the error rate and CPU usage need attention. @Hongxin Ding Your SQL optimization work can address the root cause, and I look forward to reviewing the stress test data again after optimization.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-10-22 group=Group 3 message_index=1 time=2025-10-22 09:21:10] Xinmeng Tian: Good morning everyone. Today is the last day of load testing for the data submission API. Yesterday's 100-concurrent test results showed CPU pressure. My plan for today is to directly go for 300 concurrent users to see the system's limits, providing more precise data support for Hongxin Ding's optimization.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-10-22 group=Group 3 message_index=4 time=2025-10-22 09:50:00] Huilan Chen: The plans are very clear. @Xinmeng Tian, please make sure to get the final results for the load test today, as I mentioned yesterday, this provides crucial data for performance tuning. @Hongxin Ding, your cooperation with the stress test is excellent, this closed-loop optimization approach is very good. Looking forward to your good news.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-10-22 group=Group 3 message_index=5 time=2025-10-22 11:30:00] Xinmeng Tian: @all The load test with 300 concurrent users is underway, and the situation is not looking good. The error rate has climbed to 3%, the average response time exceeds 2 seconds, and the server CPU utilization is already at 98%. @Mingzhi Li @Hongxin Ding The bottleneck is very obvious.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-10-22 group=Group 3 message_index=6 time=2025-10-22 11:32:15] Mingzhi Li: Received, this is about what I expected. @Hongxin Ding, it seems all the pressure is on you. Your SQL optimization is key. How's it going? Any leads?

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-10-22 group=Group 3 message_index=8 time=2025-10-22 12:10:00] Xinmeng Tian: Awesome! @Hongxin Ding, once you've deployed it, I can start a new round of testing anytime.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-10-22 group=Group 3 message_index=10 time=2025-10-22 16:45:20] Xinmeng Tian: @all [Good news] The load test for the data submission API interface has been completed! After Hongxin Ding's SQL optimization, a stress test with 300 concurrent users was re-conducted, and the results are very satisfactory: the average response time has dropped to 350ms, the error rate is below 0.1%, and the peak CPU usage has also fallen back to a healthy level of 65%. The performance bottleneck has been resolved! The test report has been updated and uploaded. This task has been successfully completed! 🎉

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-10-22 group=Group 3 message_index=11 time=2025-10-22 16:50:00] Mingzhi Li: Great work! @Xinmeng Tian @Hongxin Ding Perfect teamwork! One discovers problems, one solves problems, an efficient closed loop. You two worked hard.

question: In the competitive analysis task for the supply chain carbon footprint project, after Huilan Chen completed the analysis of key competitors (especially EcoTrace), which document management system did she upload the final report to?

haystack_sessions.content: [EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-27 group=Group 3 message_index=1 time=2025-01-27 09:30:00] Weihua Zhang: Good morning everyone, a new week has begun. We have a few key tasks to push forward. @Huilan Chen @Fang Mo, please synchronize the preparation progress for the interviews with the procurement and supply chain departments. Additionally, I will be starting the competitor analysis work today, and I'll need everyone's support.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-27 group=Group 3 message_index=4 time=2025-01-27 10:00:50] Weihua Zhang: Great, please continue with the interview preparation. Regarding the competitor analysis, I've initially listed several major carbon management platforms, both domestic and international. @Huilan Chen, you're experienced, please focus on the differences in their product features and user experience. @Guohua Han, you're responsible for collecting and organizing their pricing models and public market data. @Zhiyu Peng, please examine their market strategies and value propositions from a sales and customer perspective. We need to form a preliminary analysis report within this week.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-27 group=Group 3 message_index=5 time=2025-01-27 10:05:10] Huilan Chen: No problem, Boss Zhang, I'll start researching the feature matrices of these competitors right away.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-27 group=Group 3 message_index=6 time=2025-01-27 10:08:25] Guohua Han: Received, Boss Zhang. I'll start looking up pricing information and relevant research reports.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-27 group=Group 3 message_index=7 time=2025-01-27 10:10:33] Zhiyu Peng: Okay Boss Zhang, I'll also look for feedback from our potential customers about their opinions on these competitors.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-28 group=Group 3 message_index=1 time=2025-01-28 09:15:10] Weihua Zhang: Good morning, everyone. Today is January 28th, and we have two key interview tasks due today. Please, relevant colleagues, follow up on these as a priority. @Huilan Chen @Fang Mo, please synchronize the latest progress of the interview work and today's plan. In addition, the competitive product analysis work should also continue to advance.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-28 group=Group 3 message_index=6 time=2025-01-28 09:40:10] Weihua Zhang: This plan is great, @Yaying Han. Tasks should be linked up like this. Also, regarding competitor analysis, @Huilan Chen, @Guohua Han, @Zhiyu Peng, is there any initial progress on the division of labor from yesterday?

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-28 group=Group 3 message_index=7 time=2025-01-28 09:48:22] Guohua Han: Boss Zhang, I'm currently organizing the pricing models of several major competitors. I've found that most of them use tiered pricing based on the number of suppliers or total carbon emissions. The publicly available market data is quite fragmented, so I'm still gathering more industry reports for cross-validation.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-28 group=Group 3 message_index=8 time=2025-01-28 09:52:00] Huilan Chen: I'm currently looking at the features and user experience. I've initially found two competitors that do well in data visualization, but they seem to be lacking in the automation of data collection in the upstream supply chain. This could be an opportunity for us. I will continue to analyze this in depth after the interviews this afternoon.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-29 group=Group 3 message_index=3 time=2025-01-29 10:30:00] Weihua Zhang: Well done, @Yaying Han, this is the task linkage I mentioned yesterday. Also, how is the competitive analysis progressing? @Guohua Han @Huilan Chen @Zhiyu Peng, any new findings? We need to speed up; we need a preliminary report by the end of this week.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-29 group=Group 3 message_index=4 time=2025-01-29 10:38:25] Guohua Han: Boss Zhang, I've made some progress here. In addition to the tiered pricing we discussed earlier, I found a competitor called 'EcoTrace' that recently launched a 'freemium' model. It allows core enterprises to invite up to 10 suppliers to use basic features for free. This might be a strategy for them to quickly capture market share. I'm currently analyzing the potential impact of this model on our pricing.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-29 group=Group 3 message_index=5 time=2025-01-29 10:45:10] Weihua Zhang: This information is very important, @Guohua Han. The Freemium model is worth our in-depth study. @Huilan Chen, can you check what specific basic functions 'EcoTrace' includes and what the user experience is like? We need to evaluate how attractive this free model really is.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-29 group=Group 3 message_index=6 time=2025-01-29 10:50:33] Huilan Chen: Received, Boss Zhang. I'm about to research this company, and will immediately focus on analyzing their freemium version. I'll work with UX designer @Yang Zhao to look at their user onboarding process and evaluate its usability.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-29 group=Group 3 message_index=27 time=2025-01-29 15:15:45] Zhiyu Peng: Speaking of competitors, I just got off the phone with a potential client, and they also mentioned 'EcoTrace'. The client reported that while the free version's features are basic, they did successfully open up communication channels between them and several core suppliers, solving the pain point of back-and-forth email exchanges of spreadsheets. However, they also complained that the data analysis features are too weak, and they would have to upgrade to the expensive enterprise version for in-depth analysis.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-29 group=Group 3 message_index=28 time=2025-01-29 15:20:00] Weihua Zhang: Thanks to @Zhiyu Peng for the first-hand intelligence, which is very valuable. This confirms @Guohua Han's findings and our conjectures. 'EcoTrace' uses basic collaboration features as a hook and advanced data analytics as a profit point. This has strong reference significance for us in designing product value and pricing strategies.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-30 group=Group 3 message_index=1 time=2025-01-30 09:33:18] Weihua Zhang: Good morning everyone. @Huilan Chen, yesterday I mentioned that you and @Yang Zhao should focus on analyzing EcoTrace's freemium model. Do you have any initial findings? The competitor analysis needs to be sped up, and I need to see a preliminary report this week.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-30 group=Group 3 message_index=2 time=2025-01-30 09:48:05] Huilan Chen: Good morning, Boss Zhang. Yang Zhao and I have registered for and tried EcoTrace's free version. Our initial conclusion is that its user onboarding process is very streamlined and easy to get started with. However, the free features are very limited, basically only providing a basic framework for core enterprises to assign data entry tasks to suppliers, with almost no data analysis or reporting functions. Users who want to get real value must upgrade to the paid version. I am currently compiling a detailed experience analysis and screenshots, which I expect to send out this afternoon.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-30 group=Group 3 message_index=3 time=2025-01-30 09:52:30] Yang Zhao: Yes, to add to what Director Huilan Chen said, EcoTrace's UI design is quite modern, but some aspects of its interaction could be improved. Its core idea is a "hook," using a free feature that solves basic collaboration pain points to attract a large number of users, and then converting them through advanced features. This model is worth our consideration.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-30 group=Group 3 message_index=4 time=2025-01-30 10:15:11] Yaying Han: Good morning, everyone! I was just about to update you on the questionnaire's progress. I've already added a question about suppliers' current data management methods, based on Boss Jianguo Huang's suggestion yesterday. After listening to Huilan Chen and Yang Zhao's analysis, I think we can add another question to the questionnaire to explore which value-added features suppliers are willing to pay for. This way, we can link it to the competitor analysis! I'll revise it right away. 💪

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-30 group=Group 3 message_index=5 time=2025-01-30 10:20:00] Weihua Zhang: Very good. @Yaying Han, this is a great idea. Information needs to flow and be reused. @Huilan Chen, your analysis is very insightful. We'll await your detailed report this afternoon. We need to figure out how attractive EcoTrace's "hooks" really are, and where its paywall is set.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-31 group=Group 3 message_index=3 time=2025-01-31 10:00:28] Huilan Chen: @all Good morning, I have completed the analysis report on our main competitors. It focuses on EcoTrace's features, pricing, and market strategy, especially their Freemium model and user conversion path. The report has been uploaded to Confluence: https://sd.confluence.net/wiki/spaces/SCT/pages/20250131/EcoTrace+Competitive+Analysis+Report. @Weihua Zhang @Jianguo Huang @Guohua Han The core conclusion is that they use a minimalist free collaboration feature as a traffic entry point, and then set up a paywall through powerful data analysis and reporting functions. This strategy is very much worth learning from.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-31 group=Group 3 message_index=4 time=2025-01-31 10:05:10] Yang Zhao: To add to Director Huilan Chen's report, from a UI/UX perspective, EcoTrace's free version gives users a strong sense of "gain." Although the features are limited, the core pain point (getting rid of email-based spreadsheets) is solved. Once users get used to this platform, they can easily be guided to explore paid features. This "hook" is very cleverly designed.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-31 group=Group 3 message_index=5 time=2025-01-31 10:20:00] Guohua Han: The report is very detailed! @Huilan Chen, the pricing analysis in this report and the market data I collected corroborate each other. EcoTrace's enterprise version is quite expensive, which shows they are very confident in their value-added services. This can be viewed in conjunction with the willingness-to-pay survey @Yaying Han is currently conducting.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-31 group=Group 3 message_index=6 time=2025-01-31 10:45:30] Weihua Zhang: Received, @Huilan Chen, the report quality is very high, and the insights are profound. Thank you and @Yang Zhao for your in-depth analysis. EcoTrace's model indeed provides us with an important reference. It verifies that the path of "first solving collaboration efficiency, then providing data value" is feasible. This is crucial for us to define the MVP version of our product and subsequent commercialization strategies.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-31 group=Group 3 message_index=7 time=2025-01-31 16:30:00] Weihua Zhang: @all Today is January 31st, the last workday of the week, and we have achieved results on two key tasks. The competitor analysis report submitted by Huilan Chen has provided us with valuable strategic input, and I declare this analysis task completed on time. The demand questionnaire from Yaying Han has also been successfully distributed, opening a channel for us to collect user requirements. Please update the status of these tasks to "Completed." Everyone, have a great weekend!
[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-01-31 group=Group 3 message_index=9 time=2025-01-31 16:36:00] Huilan Chen: Received! Task status updated. Happy weekend, everyone!

question: After completing the analysis of the GHG Protocol and the differences across the manufacturing, retail, and energy sectors, what is the full title of the report that Peng Hou finally uploaded to Confluence?
answer: Research and Analysis Report on Mainstream Carbon Accounting Standards (GHG Protocol) - V1.0 - Final

haystack_sessions.content: [EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-04 group=Group 3 message_index=6 time=2025-02-04 14:30:00] Peng Hou: @all Today, I'm starting the research work on mainstream carbon accounting standards for this project. This work is strongly related to my research in the 'Carbon Emission Accounting and Asset Management Platform' project.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-04 group=Group 3 message_index=7 time=2025-02-04 14:32:15] Peng Hou: Therefore, I will directly reuse the foundational research results on the GHG Protocol from that side, and based on that, I will focus on analyzing parts strongly related to supply chain scenarios, such as the accounting methods and data requirements for 'Category 1: Purchased goods and services' and 'Category 4: Upstream transportation and distribution' of Scope 3 in the GHG Protocol. This avoids redundant work and makes the analysis more targeted.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-04 group=Group 3 message_index=8 time=2025-02-04 14:40:30] Mingzhi Li: This method is great, @Peng Hou. Efficient and focused. Please pay special attention to the differences in upstream data traceability practices across different industries, as this will affect our platform's data model design and whether we need to support multiple sets of accounting templates.
[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-04 group=Group 3 message_index=9 time=2025-02-04 14:45:00] Peng Hou: Understood, Boss Li. I will make this a key focus of the research.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-05 group=Group 3 message_index=5 time=2025-02-05 11:30:20] Peng Hou: Let me update you all on the progress of the research into mainstream carbon accounting standards. I've reused the foundational research on the GHG Protocol from another project, focusing on Scope 3 accounting related to supply chains. Following Boss Mingzhi Li's suggestion, I looked into the practical differences across various industries and found significant variations in upstream data traceability methods and data granularity between manufacturing and retail. Manufacturing focuses more on raw materials and components, while retail is more concerned with finished product procurement and logistics. This implies that our platform might need to offer industry-specific templates.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-05 group=Group 3 message_index=6 time=2025-02-05 11:35:40] Mingzhi Li: @Peng Hou This discovery is very important. 'Industry templates' are a key product direction. This will directly affect our data model and accounting engine design. Please make this discovery an important input for today's Roadmap meeting.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-06 group=Group 3 message_index=1 time=2025-02-06 09:15:20] Zhiyu Peng: @all Good morning everyone, today I officially begin the research and drafting of the 'Data Privacy and Compliance Strategy'. This task will involve international mainstream regulations such as GDPR, ensuring our system is compliant when processing global supply chain data. This work relies on Peng Hou's analysis of carbon accounting standards, as different standards may have different requirements for data sources and transparency. Peng Hou, when you're available, we can sync up on your findings regarding data traceability.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-06 group=Group 3 message_index=2 time=2025-02-06 09:20:45] Peng Hou: No problem, @Zhiyu Peng. I'm currently refining the differences in industry templates for manufacturing and retail, which indeed involves the depth and breadth of supplier data provision. This is directly related to privacy compliance. I'll share the first draft with you after I've organized it this afternoon, and we can find a time to discuss it in detail.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-06 group=Group 3 message_index=19 time=2025-02-06 11:10:30] Huilan Chen: Boss Zhang, the product-side milestones have been updated. Based on yesterday's meeting discussion and the industry differences mentioned by Peng Hou, I've added exploratory research on 'industry accounting templates' to the Q2 plan. This will make our product more competitive. The Confluence page has been updated.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-07 group=Group 3 message_index=2 time=2025-02-07 09:35:25] Huilan Chen: Looking forward to the final version! Boss Zhang, you've worked hard. Adding the exploration of the 'industry accounting template' makes our Q2 direction clearer, and for that, we have to thank @Peng Hou for his input.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-07 group=Group 3 message_index=3 time=2025-02-07 10:10:00] Peng Hou: You're welcome, Boss Chen. Let me update you on my progress. In my analysis of mainstream carbon accounting standards, I've found that in addition to manufacturing and retail, the energy sector's supply chain carbon footprint accounting also has its unique characteristics, especially in the upstream fuel extraction and transportation stages, where data granularity and verification methods differ. I am currently adding these differences to the analysis report.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-07 group=Group 3 message_index=4 time=2025-02-07 10:15:30] Zhiyu Peng: Good morning, @Peng Hou. Will the industry differences you mentioned affect the type and sensitivity of data we need to collect from suppliers? I am drafting a data privacy policy and need to consider these scenarios, such as whether certain data constitutes a trade secret and requires de-identification or aggregation.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-07 group=Group 3 message_index=5 time=2025-02-07 10:20:55] Peng Hou: Yes, it will have a big impact. @Zhiyu Peng, some production data from suppliers in the energy industry is very sensitive. I'll put together a brief explanation for you this afternoon, and then we can discuss it in more detail. This is very important for your strategy development.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-10 group=Group 3 message_index=1 time=2025-02-10 09:30:15] Peng Hou: @all Good morning everyone. Regarding the research and analysis report on mainstream carbon accounting standards, the part about the accounting differences in the energy industry that was mentioned last Friday has been completed. I am currently doing the final proofreading and formatting, and the final version is expected to be officially released this morning.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-10 group=Group 3 message_index=2 time=2025-02-10 09:32:45] Zhiyu Peng: Awesome! @Peng Hou, waiting for your final report. My data privacy and compliance strategy drafting work really needs the detailed analysis of data traceability and sensitivity across different industries from your report as a key input.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-10 group=Group 3 message_index=4 time=2025-02-10 09:45:10] Huilan Chen: So excited! @Hongxin Ding, the real voice of suppliers is crucial for us to define the functional priorities of the MVP version. Once you've produced the preliminary report, our product team will study it immediately. @Peng Hou, your standard analysis report is equally critical, as it determines the core capabilities of our product.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-10 group=Group 3 message_index=5 time=2025-02-10 11:30:00] Peng Hou: @all The "Mainstream Carbon Accounting Standard (GHG Protocol) Research and Analysis Report - V1.0 - Final" has been uploaded to Confluence. The report comprehensively analyzes the GHG Protocol, with a focus on comparing the differences in supply chain carbon accounting practices across manufacturing, retail, and energy industries, and their implications for our products. This research task is now complete. @Zhiyu Peng @Huilan Chen @Mingzhi Li Please check it.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-02-10 group=Group 3 message_index=6 time=2025-02-10 11:35:20] Zhiyu Peng: Received! I'll check it right away. Now I can fully roll out my work here. Thanks, @Peng Hou!

question: When discussing the design of calculation trigger APIs for the carbon emissions accounting platform, which optional parameter did Ruiqing Jiang suggest adding to flexibly calculate only specific emission categories?

haystack_sessions.content: [EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-07-17 group=Group 1 message_index=1 time=2025-07-17 10:00:15] Mingzhi Li: Good morning, everyone. Today we're officially launching a new task. Ruiqing Jiang, do you have any preliminary plans for the Scope 3 emissions calculation you're responsible for? This is a tough one.

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-07-17 group=Group 1 message_index=2 time=2025-07-17 10:05:00] Ruiqing Jiang: Hello Boss Li! Yes, I'm starting to develop the calculation logic for Scope 3 today. Scope 3 involves many categories. According to Product Manager Yu Su's PRD, we will first focus on a few key categories in this phase, such as Category 4 (upstream transportation and distribution) and Category 5 (waste generated in operations). My plan for the morning is to delve into the calculation methodologies for these two categories, especially the selection of emission factors and data source requirements. In the afternoon, I will start building the algorithm framework and implement the basic data input interface. Hehe, ready to get to work! 💪

[EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-07-17 group=Group 1 message_index=3 time=2025-07-17 10:08:30] Yu Su: @Ruiqing Jiang The plan is very clear. Regarding emission factors, we prioritize using official databases published by the state, followed by internationally recognized industry recommended values such as those from the IPCC. [EverMemBench topic=01 date=2025-07-24 group=Group 1 message_index=17 time=2025-07-24 16:25:40] Mingzhi Li: @Ruiqing Jiang This is a good question. Employees using private electric vehicles for work falls under Scope 3 for electricity consumption. However, this electricity is not purchased by the company, but rather accounted for under Category 7 (employee commuting). To simplify the model, we can currently use the officially published average grid emission factor multiplied by mileage and electricity consumption per 100 km for estimation. In terms of product design, users can be allowed to select the vehicle type, and if it's an electric vehicle, this logic can be enabled.
